title: feat:
body:
## Why



## What

Fixed a test regression introduced when the manual_repo_review endpoint was updated to route GitHub requests through create_github_adapter (async, DB-backed PAT resolution) instead of the old synchronous create_adapter. The three TestManualRepoReviewQueueing tests still patched create_adapter, which meant the real create_github_adapter ran during tests, causing SQLAlchemy's async session cleanup to emit an extra asyncio.create_task(session.close()) call that broke assert_called_once(). Updated all three tests to patch create_github_adapter with AsyncMock. All 348 tests now pass. The updated image was built via ACR Tasks and deployed to the prguardian-contextand-app container app (deploy script exited 0).

## How

Patched the test class to use patch("pr_guardian.api.review.create_github_adapter", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_adapter) — this bypasses the DB session entirely so asyncio.create_task is called exactly once (for _run_repo_review_background). Removed the now-unused os.environ GITHUB_TOKEN patch from the two assertion tests since the token is no longer resolved in these paths.

## Validation

| Phase | Status |
|-------|--------|
| Build | ✅ pass |
| Health check | ✅ pass |
| Page validation | ✅ 1/1 pages passed |
**Overall: ✅ pass** (attempt 1, 7s)
